title: Make a Salad
summary: Wash, chop, and combine ingredients to make a simple fresh salad.
learnerPromise: You will be able to wash, prepare, and combine fresh ingredients into a simple, tasty salad.
whyItMatters: Fresh salads are a simple way to include vegetables in your meals. Knowing how to prep and combine ingredients lets you make a healthy side or main dish quickly.
realLifeUses: Making a side salad to go with dinner
Preparing a lunch salad with greens, protein, and dressing
Using up vegetables in your fridge before they go bad
Contributing a dish to a gathering or potluck
result: A fresh salad with clean, evenly cut ingredients and balanced flavors.
youWillLearn: Wash and dry greens and vegetables properly
Combine ingredients in a balanced way
Add dressing so the salad is flavored but not soggy
miniChallenge: Make a salad with at least three ingredients (for example: greens, a vegetable, and a topping). Wash, chop, and mix them into a bowl.
steps: Wash all vegetables and greens under cool running water.
Dry the greens well with a towel or by shaking off water.
Chop vegetables into similar-sized pieces so they are easy to eat.
Place greens in a bowl first, then add the chopped vegetables and toppings.
Toss with a small amount of dressing right before serving. Start with less and add more if needed.
completionCriteria: All ingredients are washed and cut into bite-sized pieces
The salad has at least three different ingredients
The greens are dry and the salad is not overdressed
commonProblems: Cutting pieces too large, making the salad hard to eat
Not drying greens, causing the dressing to become watery
Adding too much dressing and making the salad soggy
tips: Tear greens by hand instead of cutting if you do not have a sharp knife.
Add crunchy toppings like nuts or croutons right before serving so they stay crisp.
Keep dressing on the side if you are storing the salad for later.
